ucs.title: [LLM] Mustard Tempest (TA569 / SocGholish) JS loader discovery burst from wscript.exe
ucs.description: Hunts the signature SocGholish post-execution behavior: a single wscript.exe instance (running the FakeUpdate .js payload extracted from a fake browser-update ZIP) spawning a tight burst of host/AD reconnaissance commands (whoami /all, nltest /domain_trusts, net group 'Domain Admins', systeminfo, ipconfig /all). This chain is what TA569 hands off to Indrik Spider before WastedLocker/LockBit deployment.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="wscript.exe" AND (Processes.parent_process="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.parent_process="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.parent_process="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") AND (Processes.process_name IN ("whoami.exe","nltest.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","hostname.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe")) by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_id Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval is_dom_recon=if(match(process,"(?i)(domain admins|domain controllers|/domain_trusts|/all|enterprise admins)"),1,0) | stats values(process_name) as recon_bins dc(process_name) as bin_variety sum(is_dom_recon) as dom_recon_hits values(process) as cmdlines min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by dest user parent_process_id parent_process | where bin_variety>=3 AND dom_recon_hits>=1 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let WindowMin = 5m;
let ReconBins = dynamic(["whoami.exe","nltest.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","hostname.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "wscript.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (".js", ".jse")
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
| where FileName in~ (ReconBins)
| extend DomainRecon = iff(ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(domain admins|domain controllers|/domain_trusts|/all|enterprise admins|trusted_domains)", 1, 0)
| summarize
    ReconBinsHit  = make_set(FileName, 50),
    BinVariety    = dcount(FileName),
    DomainReconHits = sum(DomainRecon),
    SampleCmds    = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 25),
    FirstSeen     = min(Timestamp),
    LastSeen      = max(Timestamp)
  by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountDomain, AccountName,
     InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| where BinVariety >= 3 and DomainReconHits >= 1
| where (LastSeen - FirstSeen) <= WindowMin
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: TA569's SocGholish JS loader is consistently executed by wscript.exe out of %TEMP%/Downloads after the user double-clicks a JS extracted from a fake 'Chrome/Edge/Firefox Update' ZIP. The hand-off to Indrik Spider (Evil Corp) is preceded by a near-deterministic recon block (whoami /all + nltest /domain_trusts + net group 'Domain Admins'); legitimate enterprise scripts almost never spawn that exact

ucs.title: [LLM] Mustard Tempest FakeUpdate browser-delivered ZIP→JS staging (SocGholish drive-by)
ucs.description: Detects the SocGholish delivery primitive: a browser process (chrome/msedge/firefox) writing a small ZIP with a fake-update name to the user's Downloads/Temp directory, immediately followed by extraction of a single .js with a long random/hex stem that is then opened by wscript.exe. This is the core T1189→T1204.002 chain TA569 has run on injected WordPress/CMS sites since 2017.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") AND Filesystem.file_name="*.zip" by host Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rex field=file_name "(?i)(?<lure>update|chrome|edge|firefox|browser|safari)" | where isnotnull(lure) | join type=inner dest user [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("explorer.exe","7zg.exe","7zfm.exe","WinRAR.exe","explorer.exe") AND Processes.process_name="wscript.exe" AND (Processes.process="*.js*" OR Processes.process="*.jse*") AND (Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Downloads\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process as wscript_cmd parent_process as extractor ] | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name file_name file_path extractor wscript_cmd | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let Browsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe"]);
let ZipDrops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (Browsers)
    | where FileName endswith ".zip"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
    | where FileName matches regex @"(?i)(update|chrome|edge|firefox|browser|safari|installer)"
    | project ZipTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ZipName=FileName, ZipPath=FolderPath, ZipInitiator=InitiatingProcessFileName, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let JsExec =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName =~ "wscript.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".js", ".jse")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
    | extend JsStem = extract(@"([A-Za-z0-9_\-]{8,})\.js[e]?", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
    | where isnotempty(JsStem)
    | project JsTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, JsCmd=ProcessCommandLine, JsParent=InitiatingProcessFileName, JsStem;
ZipDrops
| join kind=inner JsExec on DeviceId
| where JsTime between (ZipTime .. (ZipTime + 30m))
| project ZipTime, JsTime, DeviceName, AccountName, ZipInitiator, ZipName, ZipPath, JsParent, JsCmd, JsStem
| order by ZipTime desc
ucs.rationale: Mustard Tempest's only delivery vector is injected JS on compromised CMS sites that prompts a fake browser update; the resulting artifact is invariably a small ZIP named like Chrome.Update*.zip / Edge.Update*.zip dropped by the browser process, containing a single high-entropy .js the user executes via wscript. Tying the browser-written lure-named ZIP to a near-immediate wscript.exe JS execution o
